chunks.title: Paloma365 Delivery Module — Operations Manual
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Delivery Module — Operations Manual > Overview]
The Delivery module turns a dine-in venue into a delivery business without
third-party lock-in. It covers courier dispatch, delivery zones with
per-zone fees, live GPS tracking and native ingestion of aggregator orders
(Glovo, Wolt, Yandex Eda) into a single kitchen queue.

chunks.title: Paloma365 Delivery Module — Operations Manual
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Delivery Module — Operations Manual > Courier dispatch]
Orders enter the dispatch board the moment the kitchen bumps them. The
dispatcher assigns a courier manually or enables auto-assign, which picks
the nearest idle courier by GPS position. Each courier uses the Paloma365
Courier app; customers receive SMS or push notifications at "accepted",
"picked up" and "arriving" stages.

chunks.title: Paloma365 Delivery Module — Operations Manual
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Delivery Module — Operations Manual > Delivery zones and fees]
Zones are drawn on the map as polygons. Each zone has its own delivery fee,
minimum order value and estimated time. Orders outside all zones are
rejected at checkout with a clear message, which prevents unprofitable
long-distance runs.

chunks.title: Paloma365 Delivery Module — Operations Manual
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Delivery Module — Operations Manual > Aggregator integration]
Aggregator orders arrive through the marketplace API and appear in the same
kitchen display queue as native orders. Menu synchronisation is one-way:
Paloma365 is the source of truth, aggregator menus update within minutes.
Note that aggregator commissions are billed by the aggregators themselves
and are not part of the module subscription.

chunks.title: Paloma365 Delivery Module — Operations Manual
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Delivery Module — Operations Manual > Requirements and limitations]
The module requires at least one dedicated courier on staff. GPS tracking
requires Android 10+ on courier devices. Typical onboarding takes three to
five working days including zone setup and staff training.

chunks.title: Paloma365 Delivery Module — Operations Manual
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Delivery Module — Operations Manual > Pricing]
Setup fee 90,000 KZT, subscription 25,000 KZT per month per location.
The setup includes zone configuration, aggregator connection and two
training sessions for dispatchers.

chunks.title: Paloma365 Kitchen Display System — FAQ
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Kitchen Display System — FAQ > What is the Kitchen Display System?]
KDS replaces paper tickets with digital screens in the kitchen. Orders are
routed per station (grill, cold, dessert), each dish carries a preparation
timer, and overdue tickets flash for the expeditor. Cook-time analytics by
dish and daypart come out of the box.

metadata.heading: What is the Kitchen Display System?

chunks.title: Paloma365 Kitchen Display System — FAQ
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Kitchen Display System — FAQ > How does KDS reduce kitchen time?]
Three mechanisms. First, station routing removes ticket hand-offs: each
station sees only its own items. Second, target timers make slow dishes
visible in real time instead of at the end-of-shift report. Third,
cook-time analytics show which dishes and dayparts break the target, so
the chef can fix recipes or staffing precisely. Venues with 20+ minute
average kitchen times typically return to their target within one to two
months of adoption.

metadata.heading: How does KDS reduce kitchen time?

chunks.title: Paloma365 Kitchen Display System — FAQ
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Kitchen Display System — FAQ > What hardware is required?]
One wall-mounted display per kitchen station (any HDMI screen works) and
one Android tablet for the expeditor. Screens are not included in the
module price. A typical two-station kitchen needs about 150,000 KZT of
one-time hardware on top of the module setup fee.

chunks.title: Paloma365 Kitchen Display System — FAQ
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Kitchen Display System — FAQ > Does KDS work during internet outages?]
Yes. The kitchen queue is served from the local Paloma365 terminal; orders
keep flowing between POS and kitchen screens over the local network.
Cloud analytics resume syncing when connectivity returns.

metadata.heading: Does KDS work during internet outages?

chunks.title: Paloma365 Kitchen Display System — FAQ
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Kitchen Display System — FAQ > What does it cost?]
Setup fee 120,000 KZT, subscription 18,000 KZT per month per location.
The pilot option lets a multi-location group deploy KDS at one flagship
location first; pilot results transfer to a group-wide rollout contract.

chunks.title: Paloma365 CRM & Loyalty — Program Guide
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 CRM & Loyalty — Program Guide > What the module does]
CRM & Loyalty builds unified guest profiles from every order channel and
runs configurable loyalty mechanics on top: cashback, bonus points,
tiered discounts and birthday rewards. It is the primary tool for lifting
guest retention and repeat visit share.

chunks.title: Paloma365 CRM & Loyalty — Program Guide
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 CRM & Loyalty — Program Guide > Guest profiles]
Every order — dine-in, delivery or takeaway — is linked to a guest by
phone number. The profile keeps visit history, favourite dishes, average
ticket and channel preferences. Profiles merge automatically when a guest
uses a new phone number with the same payment card.

chunks.title: Paloma365 CRM & Loyalty — Program Guide
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 CRM & Loyalty — Program Guide > Loyalty mechanics]
Cashback accrues as bonus points, configurable per category: for example
5% on the main menu and 10% on the lunch menu. Points expire after a
configurable period (90 days by default) to encourage return visits.
Tiered programs (Silver/Gold/Platinum) unlock higher accrual rates at
spending thresholds.

chunks.title: Paloma365 CRM & Loyalty — Program Guide
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 CRM & Loyalty — Program Guide > Campaigns]
The campaign builder segments guests by RFM (recency, frequency, monetary)
and sends targeted offers. Typical campaigns: win-back for guests inactive
30+ days, birthday offers with a 7-day validity window, and double-points
happy hours to fill weak dayparts. SMS delivery is billed separately by
the telecom provider; push notifications through the guest app are free.

chunks.title: Paloma365 CRM & Loyalty — Program Guide
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 CRM & Loyalty — Program Guide > Expected impact]
Venues that launch a loyalty program with at least one active campaign
typically see retention improve within two to three months. The module's
uplift assumptions used in ROI projections are deliberately conservative:
about four percent order growth and two percent average-ticket growth.

chunks.title: Paloma365 CRM & Loyalty — Program Guide
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 CRM & Loyalty — Program Guide > Pricing]
Setup fee 60,000 KZT, subscription 20,000 KZT per month per location.

chunks.title: Paloma365 Support — Troubleshooting Guide
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Support — Troubleshooting Guide > Delivery orders not appearing in the kitchen queue]
Check three things in order. First, the aggregator connection status in
Settings, Integrations: a red badge means the marketplace token expired
and needs re-authorisation. Second, verify the venue is marked "open" in
the aggregator's own dashboard. Third, confirm the kitchen display
terminal is on the same local network as the POS. Ninety percent of cases
are the expired token.

metadata.heading: Delivery orders not appearing in the kitchen queue

chunks.title: Paloma365 Support — Troubleshooting Guide
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Support — Troubleshooting Guide > Loyalty points not accruing]
Points accrue only for orders linked to a guest profile. If the cashier
skipped the phone number prompt, the order is anonymous and earns nothing.
Enable "require guest phone for loyalty categories" in CRM settings to
prevent this. Also check that the order category participates in the
accrual rules — alcohol and tobacco are excluded by default in Kazakhstan.

chunks.title: Paloma365 Support — Troubleshooting Guide
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Support — Troubleshooting Guide > QR menu shows an outdated menu]
The QR menu caches for five minutes. If changes are older than that and
still missing, the menu was likely edited in a draft: open Menu Editor and
press Publish. Photos larger than 5 MB are rejected silently by older app
versions — keep photos between 200 KB and 2 MB.

metadata.heading: QR menu shows an outdated menu

chunks.title: Paloma365 Support — Troubleshooting Guide
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Support — Troubleshooting Guide > Courier app loses GPS during deliveries]
Battery optimisation on Android kills background GPS. Ask couriers to
exclude the Paloma365 Courier app from battery optimisation and to keep
location mode on "high accuracy". Devices older than Android 10 are not
supported for live tracking.

metadata.heading: Courier app loses GPS during deliveries

chunks.title: Paloma365 Support — Troubleshooting Guide
chunks.text:
[Paloma365 Support — Troubleshooting Guide > How to reach support]
Business hours support (09:00–21:00 Almaty time) responds within 15
minutes in the in-app chat. Critical incidents (POS down, payments
failing) have a 24/7 hotline with a one-hour resolution target. Every
venue has a dedicated success manager for non-urgent questions.
